name: Fort Ruger Market
tagline: Tiny neighborhood deli on a residential side street — Filipino plate lunches with the kind of portions only aunties serve.
mustOrder: adobo plate — pork or chicken, classic Filipino comfort
loco moco — their version is massive
parking: Street parking on Alohea Ave; residential neighborhood, usually available
talkStory: Hidden on residential Alohea Ave near Fort Ruger Park, this corner store evolved into one of Diamond Head's best-kept plate lunch secrets. The Filipino food is cooked from family recipes passed down through generations — pork adobo, lumpia, pinakbet, and dinuguan that taste like someone's lola (grandmother) made them. The same neighborhood regulars come every week. Zero tourist presence, enormous portions, and it closes early afternoon when the food runs out. Mon-Sat only.
